# nextjs16-starter-template

> Professional Next.js 16 starter template for building production-grade web applications.

This repository is a ready-to-use foundation that combines Next.js 16 (App Router), React 19, TypeScript 5 strict mode, Tailwind CSS v4, Auth.js v5, Zod v4, and Zustand v5.

## Tech Stack

- Framework: Next.js 16.1.6 (App Router, Server Components, Turbopack default)
- Runtime: React 19
- Language: TypeScript 5 (strict mode, no `any`)
- Styling: Tailwind CSS v4 (CSS-first config via `@import "tailwindcss"`)
- Auth: Auth.js v5 / NextAuth (`next-auth@beta`, package: `next-auth`)
- Validation: Zod v4
- State: Zustand v5 (client state only — not for server data)
- Testing: Vitest (unit), Playwright (E2E)
- Package Manager: pnpm

## Project Structure

- `app/` — Next.js App Router (pages, layouts, route handlers, API routes)
- `components/` — Reusable UI components (Server Components by default)
- `lib/` — Shared utilities, constants, error types, Zod schemas
- `hooks/` — Custom React hooks (client-side only)
- `store/` — Zustand client state stores
- `tests/` — Playwright E2E specs
- `types/` — Global TypeScript type definitions
- `.github/instructions/` — Copilot per-file coding instructions
- `.github/agents/` — Specialized Copilot agent definitions
- `.agents/skills/` — Reusable Copilot skill library

## Key Files

- `CLAUDE.md` — Context file for Claude and Anthropic-powered runtimes
- `AGENTS.md` — Task execution guide for AI coding agents
- `.github/copilot-instructions.md` — Main Copilot context, stack, and agent router
- `auth.ts` — Auth.js top-level config (Next.js 16 convention)
- `next.config.ts` — Next.js configuration
- `vitest.config.ts` — Vitest unit test configuration
- `playwright.config.ts` — Playwright E2E test configuration
- `proxy.ts` — **Next.js 16 official request interception file** (replaces deprecated `middleware.ts`); runs on Node.js runtime — `auth()` is callable directly here

## Conventions

- File naming: `kebab-case` for all files
- Components: Server Components by default; `"use client"` only when strictly needed
- Imports: use `@/` path alias for all internal imports
- No `any` types; strict TypeScript throughout
- All input validated with Zod at system boundaries
- Styling: semantic design tokens (`text-foreground`, `bg-card`, `border-border`, `text-muted-foreground`) defined in `app/globals.css`; prefer token classes in app UI components (docs/examples may still show `zinc-*` utilities as generic Tailwind fallbacks)
- Branching: Trunk-Based Development — `main` is always releasable

## Development Commands

```bash
pnpm dev          # Start dev server (Turbopack)
pnpm build        # Production build
pnpm test         # Vitest unit tests
pnpm test:e2e     # Playwright E2E tests (requires browser binaries: pnpm exec playwright install --with-deps chromium)
pnpm test:all     # Unit + E2E
pnpm lint         # ESLint
pnpm type-check   # TypeScript type check
```

## Quality Gate

All four commands below must pass before any task is considered done:

```bash
pnpm lint && pnpm type-check && pnpm test && pnpm build
```

## AI Infrastructure

This template includes a fully layered AI developer experience:

### Agents (`.github/agents/`)

11 specialized Copilot agents routed from `copilot-instructions.md`:

- `router.agent.md` — Default agent; classifies intent and delegates to specialists
- `feature-builder.agent.md` — Builds new features following project conventions
- `debug.agent.md` — Systematic 4-phase bug resolution
- `planner.agent.md` — Read-only architecture and implementation planning
- `code-reviewer.agent.md` — Quality, security, accessibility, and performance review
- `test-generator.agent.md` — Vitest unit tests and Playwright E2E tests
- `architect.agent.md` — System design, Mermaid diagrams, component boundaries
- `adr-generator.agent.md` — Architectural Decision Records
- `prd-creator.agent.md` — Product Requirements Documents from user stories
- `github-actions.agent.md` — CI/CD workflows and GitHub Actions
- `ui-components.agent.md` — UI component scaffolding and design system patterns

### Skills (`.agents/skills/`)

47 reusable skills covering: Next.js App Router, Auth.js, Zustand, Zod, Vitest, Playwright, Tailwind design systems, TypeScript advanced types, API design, error handling, performance, security, accessibility, git workflows, and more.

### Instruction Files (`.github/instructions/`)

18 per-file instruction files scoped with `applyTo` glob patterns:

- `nextjs.instructions.md` — App Router, Server Components, caching
- `nextjs-tailwind.instructions.md` — Tailwind CSS v4, design tokens
- `reactjs.instructions.md` — React 19 patterns, hooks, composition
- `typescript-5-es2022.instructions.md` — TypeScript strict-mode conventions
- `security-and-owasp.instructions.md` — OWASP Top 10 guidance
- `a11y.instructions.md` — WCAG 2.2 Level AA accessibility
- `performance-optimization.instructions.md` — Next.js 16/React 19 performance
- `nodejs-javascript-vitest.instructions.md` — Vitest unit testing
- `playwright-typescript.instructions.md` — Playwright E2E testing
- `context7.instructions.md` — Context7 MCP for up-to-date library docs
- `code-review-generic.instructions.md` — Structured PR review protocol
- `i18n.instructions.md` — Multi-locale routing strategies (Tier 1/2/3) and `proxy.ts` locale ordering
- `mdx.instructions.md` — MDX/Markdown rendering patterns for App Router
- `learnings.instructions.md` — `learn!` command for persisting session insights
- `markdown.instructions.md` — Markdown formatting standards
- `clean-code.instructions.md` — Naming, function design, error handling, state principles
- `feature-context.instructions.md` — Co-located invariant files for complex components
- `sonarqube_mcp.instructions.md` — SonarQube MCP usage and troubleshooting guidance

### Lifecycle Hooks (`.github/hooks/`)

3 active lifecycle hooks plus an optional `postToolUse` helper:

- `sessionStart` — Validates dev environment (Node.js ≥ 20, pnpm, node_modules, .env.local)
- `preToolUse` — Scans file-write tool payloads for hardcoded secrets before writes
- `sessionEnd` — Enforces quality gate (`pnpm lint && pnpm type-check && pnpm test && pnpm build`) before the agent finishes
- `postToolUse` helper script exists but is disabled by default (opt-in via `hooks.json`) to avoid Chat Terminal proliferation in VS Code — see `copilot-instructions.md`

### Prompt Commands (`.github/prompts/`)

30 reusable Copilot prompt files for common workflows (selected examples):

- `analyze.prompt.md` — Deep analysis of a selected file or component
- `analyze-and-generate-skills.prompt.md` — Analyze code and generate a reusable skill from it
- `check-video.prompt.md` — Summarize and extract insights from a video URL
- `create-api-route.prompt.md` — Scaffold a new Route Handler in `app/api/`
- `create-component.prompt.md` — Scaffold a new Server or Client Component
- `create-form.prompt.md` — Scaffold a new form with react-hook-form + Zod + Server Action
- `create-page.prompt.md` — Scaffold a new App Router page with layout and loading states
- `create-schema.prompt.md` — Create a Zod validation schema
- `create-store.prompt.md` — Create a Zustand client state store
- `doc-comments.prompt.md` — Add JSDoc documentation comments to a file
- `explain.prompt.md` — Explain how a selected piece of code works
- `extract-design-tokens.prompt.md` — Extract design tokens from a screenshot or mockup reference
- `fix-issue.prompt.md` — Fix a GitHub issue following project conventions
- `generate-feature-docs.prompt.md` — Generate a `.context.md` invariant file for a complex component
- `generate-tests.prompt.md` — Generate Vitest unit or Playwright E2E tests
- `health-check.prompt.md` — Run a full project health audit (lint + type-check + test + build)
- `improve-skill.prompt.md` — Improve or update an agent skill file
- `init-agent.prompt.md` — Bootstrap a Ralph Loop: create init.sh, features.json, progress.txt
- `lighthouse-audit.prompt.md` — Map Lighthouse findings to code changes
- `modify-complex-component.prompt.md` — Safely modify a component with a `.context.md` invariant file
- `no-any.prompt.md` — Replace all `any` types with proper TypeScript types
- `security-audit.prompt.md` — Run a targeted OWASP security review of auth/API routes

### MCP Servers (`.vscode/mcp.json`)

10 MCP servers pre-configured in `.vscode/mcp.json` for VS Code, plus built-in GitHub MCP support via VS Code settings:

- `@upstash/context7-mcp` — Authoritative, version-specific library documentation
- `shadcn` — shadcn/ui component registry
- `@playwright/mcp@latest` — Browser automation for testing
- `next-devtools-mcp` — Next.js DevTools integration
- `markitdown-mcp` — Convert documents (PDF, Word, Excel, HTML, images) to Markdown
- `youtube-transcript` (local) — Transcribe YouTube videos from chat
- `npm-registry` (local) — Query npm package metadata and versions
- `hacker-news` (local) — Browse and search Hacker News from chat
- `osv-vulnerability` (local) — Scan dependencies for known OSV vulnerabilities
- `rss-feed` (local) — Read and summarize RSS/Atom feeds

## Docs

- [Next.js 16 App Router](https://nextjs.org/docs/app)
- [Auth.js v5](https://authjs.dev)
- [Tailwind CSS v4](https://tailwindcss.com/docs)
- [Zod v4](https://zod.dev)
- [Zustand](https://zustand.docs.pmnd.rs)
- [Vitest](https://vitest.dev)
- [Playwright](https://playwright.dev)
